what_is_open: Determine the exact feasible welfare-regret exponent in the weak-overlap nuisance-limited regime where gamma>0 and the raw feasible exponent in thm:rate-characterization is strictly below r_star(alpha,gamma). Equivalently, prove either an improved feasible achievability result at exponent r_star, or a genuine feasible-procedure lower bound showing that every non-oracle procedure is slower.
obstruction: The frozen dependency thm:rate-characterization supplies two nonmatching facts in this regime: the full minimax converse M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over all measurable Pi-valued estimators, and an upper bound for one explicit feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of order n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)}(log n)^p with r_feas<r_star. The converse is not a lower bound for a formal subclass of 'genuinely feasible' procedures beyond the ordinary estimator class, and the core contains no separate information-theoretic restriction capturing the need to learn the weak-arm outcome regression. Conversely, the extra clipped-region drift term in the feasible upper analysis may be either intrinsic or a proof artifact; thm:rate-characterization alone does not decide which.
attempted: Using thm:rate-characterization closes the question whenever r_feas=r_star, as proved in lem:feasible-attainment-at-equal-exponents. In the strict-gap regime it only gives c n^{-r_star} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) and sup_P E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p. Trying to infer a slower feasible lower bound from the minimax converse fails because M_n is an infimum over all measurable estimators and its lower exponent r_star is weaker than the claimed slower exponent r_feas when r_feas<r_star. Trying to infer r_star-achievability also fails because the clipped-AIPW analysis still contains the weak-arm nuisance-estimation drift term.
partial_result: The exact feasible tightness question is solved in all regimes where r_feas(alpha,gamma)=r_star(alpha,gamma), including gamma=0: the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM attains n^{-r_star} up to logarithmic factors, and thm:minimax-lower rules out any faster polynomial exponent. In the remaining weak-overlap nuisance-limited regimes the strongest proved statement is the bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= sup_P E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)}(log n)^p with r_feas<r_star; whether this bracket is tight remains the isolated open problem.
